task:
Ticket 0068 — harden correctness->clarity->elegance AT THE VERIFICATION GATE, with an elegant edit that adds NO new orchestration (no new sub-agent, no per-run elegance ask, no new doc, no new cadence). This is a base-governance change under packages/personas/base/** plus its pinning test. WRITE-SCOPE for this atom: packages/personas/base/oscar.md, packages/personas/base/bob.md, packages/personas/base/plays/wrap-up.md, packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts. Do NOT touch anything else.

Three remaining teeth (Scope items 1+2 plus the pinning test; item 3 is just confirmed inline):

1) PER-ATOM VERIFY TEETH (oscar.md). Add ONE bounded, objective, LOCAL fail condition to Oscar's per-atom verify gate. Best home: a new bullet in the 'How you work' list, placed right after the ADR-0012 portability-test bullet (these are the verify-gate teeth bullets). Requirements for the wording: (a) an atom FAILS verify for *avoidable, deletable* surface INTRODUCED BY THE DIFF; enumerate the concrete triggers: a second copy of a contract that already has an owner, a redundant abstraction/wrapper, a duplicate knob/flag/alias, a deprecated shim kept 'just in case', or a name a rename would fix; (b) it is explicitly bounded to LOCAL + DELETABLE so it cannot bikeshed or slow the loop — say plainly it is NOT an open-ended 'is this elegant?', and that surface which is not local to the diff and not plainly deletable-without-losing-behavior is NOT a verify fail; (c) tie it to the shared Elegance Standard (one owner per concept; remove what does not carry weight) and make clear this is enforced BY THE VERIFIER because authors do not find their own redundancy. This single bullet also satisfies Scope item 3 (reframe): elegance reads as a gate correctness passes through at the seams that matter (one-owner, deletability), not a third-place nice-to-have. Keep it ADR-0012 PORTABLE: use only role-neutral nouns (contract, abstraction, knob, shim, rename) — NO cocoder-specific nouns — so it still teaches the role with repo nouns stripped.

2) ARCHIVE-READINESS CROSS-ATOM SWEEP (oscar.md + wrap-up.md), once per priority, NOT per run, reusing the EXISTING archive-readiness gap assessment — add NO new pass. In oscar.md's 'Priority lifecycle instinct' bullet, add that before a priority is archive-ready Oscar sweeps the priority's WHOLE FOOTPRINT for cross-atom accretion (duplicated owners, schema/abstraction creep across atoms, deletable surface) that no single atom's local verify could see — as part of the archive-readiness judgment it already performs. In wrap-up.md, extend the EXISTING gap enumeration (currently 'product behavior, architecture, tests, documentation, founder decisions, and missing evidence' — it appears in step 3 and again in the What Remains section) to also include cross-atom accretion (duplicated owners, schema/abstraction creep, deletable surface). One short clause added to the existing enumeration; do not invent a new step or section. Do NOT add a fenced block or change the founder closeout contract shape (the test pins exactly one fenced block with 10 sections in canonical order).

3) BOB POINTER (bob.md). Add AT MOST ONE line near the existing 'Elegance checkpoint' bullet (around line 25) noting that elegance is enforced at Oscar's verify gate — deletable/duplicate surface fails verify — so it is not merely self-assessed. One line only.

4) PINNING TEST (packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts). Add content test(s) inside the existing 'basePersonasDir' describe block, in the same style as the existing tests (read the file, normalize with singleLine, assert exact substrings). Pin: (a) oscar.md verify-gate elegance teeth — assert the key distinctive phrases you wrote, including 'avoidable' + 'deletable', 'second copy', a duplicate-knob phrase, 'deprecated shim', a 'rename' phrase, and the bounding 'not an open-ended' (or your exact negation phrase); (b) oscar.md archive-readiness cross-atom sweep — assert 'cross-atom accretion' and a 'whole footprint'/'once per priority' phrase; (c) wrap-up.md gap enumeration now includes 'cross-atom accretion'; (d) optionally bob.md points to the verify-gate enforcement. Assert against the EXACT strings you actually wrote.

ACCEPTANCE / what must not break: No new sub-agent, no per-run elegance ask, no new doc, no new cadence — only the existing verify gate + existing archive-readiness step are hardened, plus the one bob.md pointer line and the test. The per-atom check must stay ONE bounded question (do not add a heavy per-atom sweep). All edits to packages/personas/base/** must pass the ADR-0012 portability lens (role-neutral). EXIT CRITERION (scripted): the new content tests pass AND the existing persona/Play suites stay green — run the repo's vitest over packages/personas (base-personas.test.ts) and packages/core (plays.test.ts, prompts.test.ts, and any suite that reads these persona/Play files), plus typecheck; report the exact commands, exit codes, and the diff. Do not commit; the runner commits on Oscar's verify pass. One-shot atom (prose-judgment), not loop-amenable.
